title: Lasiorrhiza cinerea
taxonomy: Lasiorrhiza cinerea is classified within the family Lardizabalaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the climbing staff vine family. The Lardizabalaceae family is part of the order Ranunculales, which includes a variety of flowering plants. Members of the Ranunculales order are primarily found in temperate and tropical regions around the world. Lasiorrhiza cinerea's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within this group of plants.
uses: Lasiorrhiza cinerea is known to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. It is often used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for wound healing and other infections. Additionally, Lasiorrhiza cinerea is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and as an antipyretic agent. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications and benefits.
